name: invest-07-intermittent-500s
initialPrompt: We're seeing about 0.3% of API requests return 500 errors in production but we can't reproduce it locally. The errors are spread across multiple endpoints and seem to spike around traffic peaks. I need a plan to: instrument production to capture the full request/response context when a 500 occurs, identify whether it's a race condition, resource exhaustion, or a downstream dependency issue, and fix it. We also need better error reporting so we stop learning about these from user complaints.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Intermittent 500 error investigation and observability improvement

blocks.text: Reduce 500 error rate from 0.3% to < 0.01% and detect future incidents before users do
successCriteria: error detection time < 2 min via alerting

blocks.title: Add structured error logging that captures request ID, user, endpoint, and stack trace for every 500
acceptance: every 500 logged to centralized log store
log includes correlation ID linkable to trace

blocks.title: Group captured 500s by stack trace fingerprint to find the dominant failure mode
acceptance: frequency and endpoint distribution per fingerprint

blocks.question: Is the dominant 500 cause a race condition, resource exhaustion, or downstream dependency timeout?

blocks.title: Reproduce dominant failure mode in staging using load test at 2x peak traffic

blocks.description: Race conditions may not reproduce under staging load patterns different from production
blocks.mitigation: Mirror production concurrency levels; use production traffic replay if available

blocks.question: Do our downstream dependencies (payment gateway, inventory service) have documented SLOs we can alert on?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (downstream dependency SLOs) but id renamed during revise. AC-13 re-anchoring must map a comment on oq-downstream-slos to oq-dependency-slos.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'on-call', 'staging', and 'failure' with task-reproduce-staging. A comment on task-reproduce-staging must stay there, not mis-attach to task-runbook.
